national_expectations.summary: As reigning African champions (2021 AFCON) and one of the continent's highest-ranked nations, Senegal arrived in North America with genuine ambition to make a deep run and break new ground for Africa. Coverage frames this as a 'now or never' tournament for an ageing core built around Sadio Mane, Kalidou Koulibaly, Idrissa Gana Gueye and Edouard Mendy, several of whom are deep into their thirties and unlikely to return as a core in four years. Two opening defeats to France and Norway sharply lowered the bar; squeaking through as a best third-placed team is widely described as a relief and an underwhelming start relative to the side's pedigree, leaving expectations recalibrated around surviving and improving rather than the early talk of going far.

national_mood.summary: The mood swung from frustration to jubilation over the group stage. After the second loss (to Norway), reporting from Dakar captured disappointment mixed with cautious hope, with supporters lamenting defensive lapses and late substitutions while demanding a reaction. The win over Iraq, confirmed once a favourable result elsewhere in the group fell their way, turned the capital into an all-night street party: car convoys, motorbikes draped in national colours, patriotic chants and dancing until dawn. Residents described relief and renewed smiles after days of criticism, a release of tension for a qualification many had stopped expecting after the slow start.

manager_situation.summary: Coach Pape Bouna Thiaw kept the squad grounded after the Iraq rout, insisting there were still improvements to make despite the five-goal margin and stressing he could not relax until other group results secured Senegal's passage. He underlined the weight of national support and praised the team spirit of his veterans. His contractual status has been a recurring talking point: amid earlier reports of an unfinalised contract and bonus/accommodation grievances, Thiaw himself stated before the Norway match that the contract issue was settled and 'behind us'. After qualification, a former sports administrator and journalist publicly revived the debate, calling it 'incomprehensible' that administrative matters were unresolved and warning bonus questions could distract the squad. Thiaw also took an unconventional approach to the compressed schedule, sending players back to training within hours of the Iraq win rather than resting them.

historical_context.summary: Senegal's World Cup pedigree dates to their 2002 debut, when they beat reigning champions France 1-0 (Papa Bouba Diop) en route to the quarter-finals, long regarded as one of Africa's greatest World Cup moments. They returned in 2018 and reached the Round of 16 in 2022, where they were eliminated by England. On the continental stage, Senegal were crowned African champions at the 2021 Africa Cup of Nations, beating Egypt on penalties in the final, their first AFCON title. The current squad is built around that title-winning generation, including Sadio Mane, who converted the decisive penalty in 2021.

sources.outlet: Wikipedia (Senegal at the FIFA World Cup)
Wikipedia (2021 Africa Cup of Nations final)

dominant_storylines.storyline: Survival on the back of a thrashing: after two opening defeats (France 1-3, Norway 2-3), Senegal needed to win and run up a margin against Iraq, doing exactly that with a 5-0 rout aided by an early Iraqi red card, then advancing as one of the eight best third-placed teams once results elsewhere fell their way. A scrappy, fortunate route into the knockouts rather than a commanding one.

An ageing golden generation's last dance: Mane, Koulibaly, Gana Gueye and Mendy are deep into their thirties, and previews frame 2026 as a 'now or never' tournament for the core that won the 2021 AFCON, raising the stakes on a deep run before this group breaks up.

Off-field noise over the coach's status: Thiaw stated before the Norway game that his contract issue was settled and behind the team, but a day after qualification a veteran observer publicly revived the debate over unfinalised administrative and bonus matters, calling it 'incomprehensible' and warning it could unsettle the squad. The picture is contested rather than a confirmed crisis.

Coach keeps the brakes on the celebration: despite the five-goal win, Thiaw publicly insisted improvements were still needed and sent players back to training within hours, signalling a focus on the harder tests to come rather than the emphatic scoreline.

R32 opponent now confirmed by the Senegalese press as Belgium (not England): Wiwsport and Seneweb report the Lions will meet the Group G winners on Wednesday 1 July in Seattle, with the winner facing the USA-Bosnia survivor. Crucially, the national press explains the pairing flipped late: DR Congo's comeback on the final group day reshuffled the bracket, moving Senegal off a projected England matchup and onto Belgium. This resolves the conflicting England/Belgium/Congo reports flagged in the existing file.
dominant_storylines.verification: attributed_context_only — opponent cross-checks with DB fact 'R32 vs a group winner' (Belgium won Group G); DB trusted on any conflict.

dominant_storylines.storyline: 'Tableau d'enfer' — a bracket from hell: with qualification secured, Wiwsport frames Senegal's knockout path as brutal, Belgique now, then potentially the USA or Bosnia, and beyond that a quarter loaded with Spain, Portugal, Croatia, France, Germany or Morocco. The outlet calls it 'un parcours de combattant' to reach any final dream, recasting the relief of qualifying into the reality of one of the toughest possible roads.

The 'spectre de Bata' — facing their own demons: ahead of the must-win Iraq game, Seneweb framed the night through the trauma of the 2012 AFCON in Bata, Equatorial Guinea, when a star-laden Senegal (Demba Ba, Moussa Sow, Papiss Cissé) crashed out with three straight 1-2 defeats in one of the federation's biggest fiascos. After the Norway loss the local press argued the fear of reliving that disillusionment against Iraq was very real, a psychological framing the 5-0 win ultimately exorcised.

How the Iraq rout was won — a super-sub and rewarded rotation: Wiwsport's player ratings credit Pape Gueye as the catalyst off the bench (a brace plus an assist after coming on), Iliman Ndiaye's lively entrance, and Thiaw's lineup changes that 'bore fruit from the opening minutes'. The flip side: much-anticipated Ibrahim Mbaye was rated the least involved player on the pitch, and Mané, though still without a goal in the tournament, was judged active and decisive in drawing the Iraqi red card.

A tactical autopsy from an ex-Lions coach: former national-team manager Amara Traoré, interviewed by L'Observateur (via Seneweb), argued Senegal controlled France with a disciplined zone for an hour before the structure 'fell apart' from the 60th minute, blaming the goals on missing pressing, poor preventive defence and midfield disorganisation. His prescription, sustain the first-hour level across the full match, became a reference point in the national tactical debate after the group-stage stumbles.

attributed_quotes.quote: Even though we scored five goals, there are still improvements to be made.
attributed_quotes.context: Thiaw speaking after Senegal's 5-0 win over Iraq, keeping expectations measured despite the big margin while awaiting other results to confirm qualification. Quote independently corroborated verbatim across Geo Super, Tribune India, Vodacom Soccer and Daily Sun.

attributed_quotes.quote: Maintenant, on a digere et l'equipe a faim d'aller chercher la victoire pour le match de demain.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Maintenant, on a digere et l'equipe a faim d'aller chercher la victoire pour le match de demain.
attributed_quotes.context: On the eve of the must-win Iraq match, after back-to-back defeats to France and Norway. Translation: 'Now we have processed it and the team is hungry to go and seek victory for tomorrow's match.' Verbatim confirmed via direct WebFetch of the URL.

attributed_quotes.quote: There won't be an anti-Haaland plan, there will be an anti-Norway plan.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Il n'y aura pas de plan anti-Haaland, il y aura un plan anti-Norvège.
attributed_quotes.context: At his pre-match press conference on the eve of Senegal vs Norway, Thiaw stressed his side would defend Norway as a collective rather than build a player-specific scheme around Erling Haaland. Verbatim confirmed via direct WebFetch of the Dakaractu URL and corroborated verbatim by APS (Senegal's national news agency).

attributed_quotes.quote: For 60 minutes, the zone defence was well in place. But from the 60th minute, our block fell apart.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Pendant 60 minutes, la défense en zone était bien en place. Mais à partir de la 60e minute, notre bloc s'est disloqué.
attributed_quotes.context: Traoré, interviewed by L'Observateur (relayed by Seneweb), diagnosing where Senegal lost control against France and what to fix before Norway. Verbatim confirmed via direct WebFetch of the Seneweb article; single-source, so flagged discovery/attributed.

gaps: R32 opponent: DB FACTS state TBD, so next_opponent_code is set to TBD. Press sources actively contradict each other: Dakar/French coverage (Afrik.com, France 24 fr) frames a likely England matchup, some English-language aggregations name Belgium, and one aggregator even named Congo DR. Trusted the DB and did not lock an opponent; re-check once the Group G/the relevant winner is confirmed.

No confirmed or reported player injury / suspension / availability news for the R32 was found in credible sources, so local_availability_reporting is empty. Worth a targeted re-check closer to the match (e.g., yellow-card accumulation or knocks from the group stage).

No official FIFA, CAF or Senegalese FA (FSF) press statement was successfully retrieved (FIFA team-profile fetch returned no usable content; only a third-party-relayed FIFA social post about qualification was seen). All sourced sections rest on press/discovery tier; an official-tier confirmation of the squad/manager situation is missing.

Manager contract situation is contested rather than settled news: Thiaw himself said pre-Norway it was resolved ('Le probleme du contrat est regle... c'est derriere nous', via Africa Top Sports), while a commentator (Mbaye Jacques Diop on iRadio, via SeneNews) revived the debate post-qualification. No federation on-record response or fully independent confirmation of an ongoing dispute was obtained; SeneNews article is partly paywalled. Storyline softened accordingly.

One aggregated search result claimed Senegal 'won two recent AFCON titles'; the verified record is a single AFCON title (2021), so only that is stated. The double-title claim was treated as an error and excluded.

France 24 (en and fr), ANI and Dakaractu article pages returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch. The ANI 'improvements to be made' quote was confirmed verbatim via four corroborating outlets (Geo Super, Tribune India, Vodacom Soccer, Daily Sun) so it stands at quote grade; the Dakaractu French verbatim quote was confirmed via successful WebFetch of that URL.
No verbatim on-record quote from a current Senegal player (e.g., Mane, Koulibaly) was captured at exact <=20-word grade; player sentiment is paraphrased in previews only.

source_data_snapshot.used_for: Confirms R32 opponent = Belgium (Group G winners), Wed 1 July, Seattle, 20:00 GMT; winner faces USA/Bosnia. Verified via WebFetch.

Second national-press confirmation of Senegal-Belgium R32; verbatim notes DR Congo's comeback reshuffled the bracket from a projected England tie to Belgium. Resolves the file's England/Belgium/Congo gap. Verified via WebFetch.
'Tableau d'enfer' bracket-path framing: Belgium then USA/Bosnia, then Spain/Portugal/Croatia/France/Germany/Morocco possible; 'parcours de combattant' phrasing confirmed via WebFetch.

Player-ratings/tactical read of the 5-0 Iraq win: Pape Gueye super-sub brace+assist, Thiaw's rotation rewarded, Iliman Ndiaye's impact, Ibrahim Mbaye flat (lowest rating), Mané active and drew the red card but still goalless. Verified via WebFetch (stat numbers left out of text).
'Spectre de Bata' psychological framing pre-Iraq: 2012 AFCON Bata fiasco (three 1-2 defeats vs Zambia/Equatorial Guinea/Libya) invoked. Verified via WebFetch.
Amara Traoré (ex-Lions coach) tactical autopsy of the France defeat via L'Observateur; verbatim 'bloc s'est disloqué' quote and the full-match prescription. Verified via WebFetch.

Post-Norway Mané sentiment (refuses pessimism, still believes in a small chance) — paraphrase only, no usable verbatim quote captured; NOT used for a quote.
